Package global.thalion.ttio.providers

Interface StorageDataset

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
public interface StorageDataset extends AutoCloseable
One 1-D typed array or compound-record array.

API status: Stable (Provisional per M39 — may change before v1.0).

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIOStorageDataset, Python ttio.providers.base.StorageDataset.

Since:
0.6

  • Method Details

    • name

      String name()

    • precision

      Enums.Precision precision()
      Primitive precision, or null for compound datasets (use compoundFields() there).

    • shape

      long[] shape()
      Full shape tuple. 1-D datasets return {N}.

    • length

      default long length()
      Size along the first axis (= shape()[0]).

    • chunks

      default long[] chunks()
      Chunk shape, or null for contiguous storage.

    • compoundFields

      List<CompoundField> compoundFields()
      Compound field schema, or null for primitive datasets.

    • readAll

      Object readAll()
      Read all elements.

      Return type varies by backend (Appendix B Gap 2):

      • Primitive datasets: native Java array matching precision() — double[] for FLOAT64, int[] for INT32/UINT32, long[] for INT64, float[] for FLOAT32, byte[] (native packing) for COMPLEX128.
      • Compound datasets — HDF5: List<Object[]> of row values in field-order.
      • Compound datasets — SQLite / non-typed backends: List<Map<String, Object>> keyed by field name.

      For backend-agnostic row iteration call readRows() — it normalises both compound shapes into a uniform list of field-keyed maps.

    • readSlice

      Object readSlice(long offset, long count)
      Hyperslab read: count elements starting at offset. Same return-type rules as readAll().

    • writeAll

      void writeAll(Object data)

    • readRows

      default List<Map<String,Object>> readRows()
      Read a compound dataset as a uniform List<Map<String, Object>> regardless of backend. Default implementation converts the HDF5 List<Object[]> shape; SQLite and other map-backed providers pass through.

      Throws IllegalStateException if called on a primitive dataset.

      Appendix B Gap 2 — backend-agnostic compound access.

    • readCanonicalBytes

      default byte[] readCanonicalBytes()
      Return the dataset contents as a byte stream in the TTIO canonical layout (v0.7 M43).

      Semantics:

      • Primitive numeric: little-endian packed values.
      • Compound: rows in storage order; fields in declaration order. VL strings as u32_le(length) || utf-8_bytes. Numeric fields little-endian.

      Signatures and encryption consume this so a signed or encrypted dataset verifies identically regardless of which provider wrote it. Default implementation handles Hdf5DatasetAdapter's native arrays and SqliteDataset's list-of-maps; providers with a zero-copy fast path may override.

    • canonicaliseCompoundRows

      static byte[] canonicaliseCompoundRows(List<Map<String,Object>> rows, List<CompoundField> fields)
      Since:
      0.7 — helper exposed for providers that want to override readCanonicalBytes() but share the compound path.

    • canonicalisePrimitive

      static byte[] canonicalisePrimitive(Object raw, Enums.Precision p)
      Since:
      0.7 — helper for the primitive canonical path.

    • hasAttribute

      boolean hasAttribute(String name)

    • getAttribute

      Object getAttribute(String name)

    • setAttribute

      void setAttribute(String name, Object value)

    • deleteAttribute

      void deleteAttribute(String name)
      Remove an attribute. No-op if the attribute does not exist. Appendix B Gap 8 — added for parity with Python ABC and ObjC -deleteAttributeNamed:error:.

    • attributeNames

      List<String> attributeNames()
      List attribute names. Returns an empty list if there are no attributes. Appendix B Gap 8.

    • close

      default void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
